title: chore(quest): simplify quest approval and payout
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the quest board
gist.blurb: We've swept away over 600 lines of bespoke automation for our community quests, leaning on GitHub's native tools instead. Payouts for merged quests are still automatic, just a whole lot simpler under the hood.
gist.summary: Simplified the community quest approval and payout workflows by replacing custom automation with GitHub-native label permissions.
gist.impact: Contributors will still receive automatic Pollen payouts for merged quests, but the underlying system is now much lighter and easier to maintain.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee (Polly) cleaning up a wooden quest board or sweeping away old code/gears. The Nomnom creature eating the deleted code (representing the 600+ lines removed).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot sweeping away old gears and code snippets from a wooden quest board, while the round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of deprecated scripts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm cream gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
